Why does our pytest suite take 4 minutes in CI but 40 seconds locally?

Most likely causes: (1) CI runners are slower and have cold caches; (2) your CI invokes pytest without -n auto so tests run serially while locally you use pytest-xdist; (3) the CI database is provisioned per-run, so fixtures that create schema pay that cost every time. Check if pytest-xdist is in the CI command and consider caching the .pytest_cache and pip wheels.
Should we migrate from pip/requirements.txt to poetry?

It's optional. Poetry gives you lockfiles and better dependency resolution, but pip-tools achieves most of that with less churn. If the team is happy with requirements.txt + constraints, there's no urgent reason to migrate. Parking this.

Why does our pytest suite take 4 minutes in CI but 40 seconds locally?

Most likely causes: (1) CI runners are slower and have cold caches; (2) your CI invokes pytest without -n auto so tests run serially while locally you use pytest-xdist; (3) the CI database is provisioned per-run, so fixtures that create schema pay that cost every time. Check if pytest-xdist is in the CI command and consider caching the .pytest_cache and pip wheels.
Should we migrate from pip/requirements.txt to poetry?

It's optional. Poetry gives you lockfiles and better dependency resolution, but pip-tools achieves most of that with less churn. If the team is happy with requirements.txt + constraints, there's no urgent reason to migrate. Parking this.

Here's our Dockerfile, keep it in mind:

```dockerfile
FROM python:3.11-slim AS base
ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
WORKDIR /app
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential libpq-dev && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
COPY . .
RUN useradd -m appuser && chown -R appuser /app
USER appuser
EXPOSE 8000
CMD ["gunicorn", "-w", "4", "-b", "0.0.0.0:8000", "app.wsgi:application"]
```
Noted — python:3.11-slim, gunicorn with 4 workers, non-root user. Looks standard.
Why does our pytest suite take 4 minutes in CI but 40 seconds locally?

Most likely causes: (1) CI runners are slower and have cold caches; (2) your CI invokes pytest without -n auto so tests run serially while locally you use pytest-xdist; (3) the CI database is provisioned per-run, so fixtures that create schema pay that cost every time. Check if pytest-xdist is in the CI command and consider caching the .pytest_cache and pip wheels.
Should we migrate from pip/requirements.txt to poetry?

It's optional. Poetry gives you lockfiles and better dependency resolution, but pip-tools achieves most of that with less churn. If the team is happy with requirements.txt + constraints, there's no urgent reason to migrate. Parking this.

Unrelated but for context, our audit tables:

```sql
CREATE TABLE audit_log (
    id bigint GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY PRIMARY KEY,
    actor_id bigint,
    action text NOT NULL,
    entity text NOT NULL,
    entity_id bigint,
    diff jsonb,
    at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_entity ON audit_log (entity, entity_id, at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_actor ON audit_log (actor_id, at DESC);
```
